cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. The question is asking for the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. Let me break it down step by step. First, I need to determine today's date. Jane was born on the last day of February. Now, February typically has 28 days, except in leap years when it has 29. Since 2001 is not a leap year (because leap years are divisible by 4, and 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so no), the last day of February 2001 would be February 28, 2001. So Jane's birthday is 02/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is her birthdate plus 16 years. Adding 16 years to 2001 gives 2017. So today's date should be February 28, 2017. Let me confirm that. If she was born on Feb 28, 2001, then each subsequent birthday would be on Feb 28 of each year, unless there's some issue with leap years. But since 2001 isn't a leap year and the birthday is on the 28th, it's straightforward. So today is 02/28/2017. Now the question asks for the date one year ago from today. So if today is 02/28/2017, then one year ago would be 02/28/2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year (divisible by 4, 2016/4=504, so yes). However, the question is not about Jane's birthday in 2016, but the date one year prior to today (02/28/2017). So regardless of whether 2016 is a leap year, one year before 02/28/2017 is 02/28/2016. However, there's a catch here. If we go back one year from 2017, do we have to consider if there's a different date? Wait, if today is her 16th birthday, that would mean that today is the day she turns 16. So if she was born on Feb 28, 2001, then her 16th birthday would be on Feb 28, 2017. So if today is Feb 28, 2017, then one year ago from today would be Feb 28, 2016. But let me check the options. Option C is 02/28/2016, which is one of the options. But wait, I'm confused because sometimes when people say "one year ago," they might mean the same day last year. But if today is Feb 28, 2017, then yes, one year ago would be Feb 28, 2016. However, if today was March 1, 2017, then one year ago would be March 1, 2016. But in this case, since today is exactly on the birthday, which is Feb 28, 2017, then one year ago is indeed Feb 28, 2016. So option C is the correct answer. Looking at the options, (C) is 02/28/2016. Let me check the other options to make sure. Option A is 02/28/2015. If today is 2017, then two years ago would be 2015. Option B is March 31, 2016, which doesn't seem related. Option D is 02/28/1992, which is way too early. Option E and F are March dates in 2016, which again don't fit. So option C is the correct one.
